title: Fix shell-to-Python injection in scientific-paper-searcher URL encoding
description:
Four scripts interpolate shell variables directly into a Python string via single-quote expansion, breaking on queries containing single quotes (e.g. "Parkinson's disease").

## Affected locations (verified line numbers)

1. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-pubmed line 33
   ENCODED_QUERY=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.quote('$QUERY'))" ...)

2. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-arxiv line 32
   Same pattern as #1.

3. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-crossref line 34
   Same pattern as #1.

4. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/lookup-s2 lines 47, 56, 59
   Different variant — uses triple-single-quotes and ${PAPER_ID} instead of $QUERY:
   Line 47: ENCODED_ID=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.quote('''${PAPER_ID}''', safe=':'))" ...)
   Lines 56, 59: ENCODED_QUERY=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.quote('''${PAPER_ID}'''))" ...)
   NOTE: Line 47 uses safe=':' which must be preserved in the fix.

search-s2 line 42 already uses the safe sys.argv pattern:
  ENCODED_QUERY=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse, sys; print(urllib.parse.quote(sys.argv[1]))" "$QUERY")

## Fix

For search-pubmed (line 33), search-arxiv (line 32), search-crossref (line 34):
  ENCODED_QUERY=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse, sys; print(urllib.parse.quote(sys.argv[1]))" "$QUERY" 2>/dev/null || echo "$QUERY" | sed 's/ /+/g')

For lookup-s2 line 47 (preserve safe=':'):
  ENCODED_ID=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse, sys; print(urllib.parse.quote(sys.argv[1], safe=':'))" "$PAPER_ID" 2>/dev/null || echo "$PAPER_ID")

For lookup-s2 lines 56 and 59:
  ENCODED_QUERY=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse, sys; print(urllib.parse.quote(sys.argv[1]))" "$PAPER_ID" 2>/dev/null || echo "$PAPER_ID" | sed 's/ /+/g')

## Acceptance criteria

1. ./scripts/search-pubmed "Parkinson's disease" --limit 1 returns valid JSON with results
2. ./scripts/search-arxiv "it's a test" --limit 1 returns valid JSON
3. ./scripts/search-crossref "women's health" --limit 1 returns valid JSON
4. rg "urllib.parse.quote\('" skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/ returns zero matches
5. rg "'''\$" skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/lookup-s2 returns zero matches

title: Implement YouTube comment search sensor in sm-video
description:
Implement the first real read-only sm-video sensor after the scaffold exists: YouTube comment retrieval and search. The command fetches comments through the yt-dlp executable, normalizes them to the shared Comment schema, and returns the standard JSON envelope.

Expected path:
- skills/video-research/scripts/sm-video, unless skillmonger-k74.2 documents a different executable location.

Start here:
- docs/video-research-direction.md sections Envelope, Comment schema, First Feature, Prerequisites, and Guardrails.
- bd show skillmonger-k74.2 --json for the CLI scaffold and envelope contract.
- bd show skillmonger-k74.7 --json for raw yt-dlp fixture expectations.
- Existing examples: skills/youtube-search/scripts/search for field masks and envelope style. Do not copy its legacy python3 -m yt_dlp invocation.

Discovery:
- rg -n "Comment|comments|write-comments|get-comments|yt-dlp|filter_fields|build_envelope" docs skills scripts -g "!vendor/**"
- yt-dlp --help | rg -n "write-comments|get-comments|dump-json|skip-download"

Scratchpad:
Use /private/tmp/sm-video-comments/ for raw local experiments. Commit only small sanitized fixtures. Put durable limitations in docs/video-research-direction.md or the fixture manifest.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance:
- Depends on skillmonger-k74.2 and should use fixtures or findings from skillmonger-k74.7.
- sm-video comments --describe returns structured command metadata.
- sm-video comments --url URL --limit N --offset N --sort top|new returns the standard envelope.
- Optional --query TEXT filters normalized comment text case-insensitively.
- --fields filters returned Comment objects without changing envelope meta.
- Non-YouTube URLs fail with a clear unsupported_provider or unsupported_url envelope; BiliBili and generic comments are deferred.
- The implementation invokes yt-dlp executable paths only, with no python3 -m yt_dlp.
- The command does not write media files, credential files, or persistent comment dumps during normal operation.
- yt-dlp failures, malformed fixture data, invalid sort values, invalid field names, and malformed URLs all return failure envelopes.

Testing:
- Add offline tests using sanitized raw comment fixtures.
- Test normalization into Comment fields: id, text, author, author_id, timestamp, like_count, parent_id, is_reply, reply_count, source_url, provider.
- Test limit and offset after normalization and query filtering.
- Test query filtering is case-insensitive.
- Test --fields masks Comment data.
- Test invalid sort, invalid field, non-YouTube URL, malformed fixture, and simulated yt-dlp failure.
- Run the focused video-research test command and scripts/validate-skill.sh skills/video-research/.

Out of scope:
- BiliBili comments or danmaku.
- Transcript extraction.
- Downloads, clips, explore pages, or other actuators.
- Rewriting existing youtube skills.

title: Scaffold sm-video CLI contract and prereq checks
description:
Scaffold only the initial video-research skill and sm-video CLI contract. This ticket creates the runnable skeleton, JSON envelope helper, schema introspection, prerequisite checks, boundary validation, field masks, and tests. It does not implement live provider operations beyond validation and not_implemented envelopes.

Expected paths:
- skills/video-research/SKILL.md
- skills/video-research/CONFIG.yaml
- skills/video-research/MEMO.md
- skills/video-research/scripts/sm-video
- skills/video-research/scripts/check-prereqs.sh
- skills/video-research/tests/ or skills/video-research/fixtures/

Start here:
- docs/video-research-direction.md lines for Naming, Sensors And Actuators, Envelope, Prerequisites, and Guardrails.
- AGENTS.md for skill validation and landing workflow.
- Existing examples: skills/youtube-search/scripts/search and skills/youtube-search/scripts/deep-dive for envelope and --describe patterns. Treat their python3 -m yt_dlp usage as legacy, not a pattern for new code.

Discovery:
- rg -n "build_envelope|--describe|filter_fields|check-prereqs|yt-dlp|python3 -m yt_dlp" skills docs scripts -g "!vendor/**"
- rg --files | rg "SKILL.md|CONFIG.yaml|check-prereqs|tests|fixtures"

Scratchpad:
Use docs/video-research-scratchpad.md for temporary implementation notes if needed. Do not edit deployed copies under .claude/skills or yolobox directories.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance:
- skills/video-research validates with scripts/validate-skill.sh skills/video-research/.
- sm-video --describe returns structured metadata for sensors search, probe, transcript, comments, sites and actuators download, clip, explore.
- sm-video --describe identifies actuator commands as side-effecting and requiring --dry-run before execution.
- Every command path returns the standard envelope with success, data, and meta.
- Unimplemented commands return success:false with meta.error.code set to not_implemented.
- Global options include --provider, --limit, --offset, --fields, and --pretty where applicable.
- Actuator options include --dry-run and CWD-sandboxed output arguments.
- check-prereqs reports yt-dlp as required and ffmpeg as optional with required_for capabilities.
- The scaffold rejects invalid provider values, control characters, embedded ? or # in identifiers, percent-encoded resource names, absolute output paths, and traversal paths.
- New code invokes yt-dlp executable paths only; no python3 -m yt_dlp.

Testing:
- Add focused scaffold tests for --describe, envelope success/failure shape, field mask parsing, invalid provider handling, unsafe path rejection, and not_implemented command behavior.
- Test check-prereqs with mocked or PATH-controlled yt-dlp and ffmpeg availability.
- Run the focused video-research tests plus scripts/validate-skill.sh skills/video-research/.

Out of scope:
- Live YouTube or BiliBili provider calls.
- Comment search implementation.
- Media download or clip execution.
- Migration of existing youtube skills.

title: Add quality-shaped results to youtube-search for small limits
description:
## Goal

Add a `--quality-rank` flag to the search script that, for small result sets (limit <= 5), fetches extra candidates, enriches each with deep-dive metadata, scores them by quality signals, and returns the top N ranked results.

## Files to change

1. `skills/youtube-search/scripts/search` (Python, ~232 lines)
   - Argparse setup: lines 97-107 — add `--quality-rank` flag
   - yt-dlp command build: lines 139-148 — when quality-rank active, expand `--playlist-end` to `min(limit * 3, 20)`
   - Result output: lines 217-227 — after building data array, add quality-ranking block

2. `skills/youtube-search/scripts/evaluate` (Python, ~251 lines)
   - `score_single_video()`: line 26 — reuse this scoring logic (import or extract to shared module)
   - Quality signals it uses: views_per_day (line 34), like_ratio (line 58), channel_followers (line 69), chapters (line 77), captions (line 87), heatmap (line 97)
   - Do NOT modify this script — import or copy the ~30-line scoring function

3. `skills/youtube-search/scripts/deep-dive` (Python, ~190 lines)
   - Call per video via subprocess. Uses `--dump-json` (not flat-playlist) so returns like_count, channel_followers, chapters, heatmap, captions.
   - Do NOT modify this script

4. `skills/youtube-search/SKILL.md`
   - Workflow "### 3. Deduplicate and Rank" (line 47): document `--quality-rank` flag
   - Usage examples (lines 30-33): add example with `--quality-rank`

5. `skills/youtube-search/CONFIG.yaml` — bump version to 0.2.0

## Implementation plan

In `scripts/search`:

1. Add argparse flag: `--quality-rank` (store_true)
2. When `--quality-rank` and `limit <= 5`: expand internal fetch to `min(limit * 3, 20)` by adjusting `--playlist-end`
3. For each candidate in expanded set, call deep-dive via subprocess:
   `subprocess.run(["python3", "scripts/deep-dive", video_id, "--pretty"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)`
4. Score each enriched video using `score_single_video()` logic from evaluate
5. Sort by score descending, trim to original limit
6. Add `"quality_ranked": true` to output envelope `meta`

Import strategy: copy `score_single_video` and `days_since` (lines 15-107 of evaluate) into search or a new `scoring.py` in the same directory.

## API boundary

- New `meta.quality_ranked` boolean field in output envelope — backward-compatible
- `--quality-rank` is opt-in, no change to default behavior
- SKILL.md workflow section is a public interface — must update documentation

## Performance note

Each deep-dive call takes ~2-5 seconds. For limit=3 with 3x over-fetch = 9 deep-dives, expect ~18-45 seconds total. Acceptable for the "high quality few results" use case. Print progress to stderr (not stdout) to avoid corrupting JSON output.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `scripts/search "photosynthesis" --limit 3 --quality-rank --pretty | jq '.meta.quality_ranked'` → `true`
2. Each result contains deep-dive fields (`like_count`, `channel_followers`, `chapters`) NOT present in plain search
3. `scripts/search "photosynthesis" --limit 10 --quality-rank --pretty | jq '.meta.quality_ranked'` → absent (flag ignored for limit > 5)
4. `scripts/search "photosynthesis" --limit 3 --pretty | jq '.meta.quality_ranked'` → absent (no regression without flag)
5. `scripts/validate-skill.sh skills/youtube-search/` passes
6. `scripts/search "photosynthesis" --limit 3 --quality-rank | scripts/evaluate` — produces valid scored output

## Out of scope

- Do NOT change evaluate script behavior or scoring formula
- Do NOT change deep-dive script
- Do NOT add --quality-rank to the youtube orchestrator skill (follow-up)
- Do NOT modify --filter, --date, or other existing flags
close_reason: Implemented --quality-rank for youtube-search, documented it, bumped version, and QA passed validate plus live search/evaluate acceptance checks.

title: Harden scientific-paper-searcher quality signals for limit=N
description:
## Goal

The orchestrator `scripts/search` concatenates results from multiple databases but applies no final sort — it just deduplicates and returns results in database-iteration order. When a user passes `--limit N`, the final N results should be ranked by citation count (descending) so high-impact papers appear first, regardless of which database returned them.

## Files to change

1. `skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search` (the orchestrator)
   - Lines 20-25: PASSTHROUGH_ARGS — already forwards `--min-citations`, `--fields-of-study`, `--venue`, `--sort` to sub-scripts. No changes needed here.
   - Lines 85-88: The dedup block. After dedup, add a sort by `.citations` descending.
   - Current dedup jq: `group_by(if .doi then .doi else .source_id end)[] | .[0]`
   - After dedup, pipe through: `sort_by(.citations // 0) | reverse`
   - The `// 0` is needed because PubMed and arXiv set citations to null.

2. `skills/scientific-paper-searcher/SKILL.md`
   - Update the orchestrator documentation to note that merged results are sorted by citation count.

## Context (pre-verified)

- S2 filter passthrough (min-citations, fields-of-study, venue) already works — each per-database script silently ignores unknown flags. Do NOT re-add these.
- Default database set is `pubmed,crossref` — PubMed is published literature, NOT preprints. arXiv/bioRxiv are preprints but are NOT in the default set.
- Only `search-s2` defaults to `citationCount:desc` (line 50). PubMed uses relevance, CrossRef uses `sort=relevance`.

## API boundary

This changes the output ORDER of `scripts/search` for multi-database queries. Output schema and field names are unchanged. This is a behavioral change to a public script documented in SKILL.md.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `./scripts/search "attention mechanism" --databases s2,pubmed --limit 5 | jq '.results | map(.citations // 0)'` — results array sorted with highest citations first.
2. `./scripts/search "attention mechanism" --databases s2,pubmed --limit 5 | jq '[.results[] | .citations // 0] | . as $a | all(range(1; length); $a[.] <= $a[.-1])'` — returns `true` (monotonically non-increasing).
3. Results with `citations: null` (from PubMed/arXiv) sort to the end.
4. Single-database behavior unchanged: `--databases s2` alone returns results in S2's native order.
5. `scripts/validate-skill.sh skills/scientific-paper-searcher/` passes.

## Out of scope

- Dedup preferring published over preprint versions (separate ticket if needed)
- Adding `--min-citations` or `--fields-of-study` to the orchestrator (already supported via passthrough)
- Changing default `--databases` list
close_reason: Merged multi-database scientific-paper-searcher results now sort by citation count after dedup while single-database order is preserved; validate and live jq acceptance checks passed.

title: Characterize yt-dlp outputs for sm-video facade
description:
Characterize representative raw yt-dlp outputs before building normalization logic. This is a characterization-only ticket: capture sanitized fixture shapes and document current upstream behavior. Do not require sm-video normalization here unless the scaffold already exists and a narrow offline smoke test is trivial.

Scope:
- YouTube metadata raw JSON.
- YouTube comments raw JSON from yt-dlp --write-comments behavior.
- BiliBili metadata raw JSON.
- BiliBili danmaku/subtitle/comment behavior only where public unauthenticated yt-dlp support is confirmed. If unavailable, document the limitation instead of forcing implementation.

Start here:
- docs/video-research-direction.md for schemas VideoResult, Comment, TimedTextSegment, Prerequisites, and Guardrails.
- bd show skillmonger-k74.2 --json for the future scaffold contract.
- yt-dlp --help sections for --dump-json, --dump-single-json, --write-comments, --skip-download, --list-subs, and --sub-langs.

Discovery:
- yt-dlp --version
- yt-dlp --list-extractors | rg -i "youtube|bili|bilibili"
- rg -n "VideoResult|Comment|TimedTextSegment|write-comments|dump-json|yt-dlp" docs skills scripts -g "!vendor/**"

Scratchpad:
Collect raw temporary outputs under /private/tmp/video-research-fixtures/. Before committing fixtures, sanitize private or variable data and keep files small. Put findings in a manifest next to fixtures.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance:
- Sanitized offline fixtures exist for YouTube metadata, YouTube comments, BiliBili metadata, and either BiliBili timed-text/danmaku data or a documented limitation note.
- A fixture manifest records provider, purpose, public source URL or redacted note, yt-dlp version, collection command, collection date, known missing fields, and volatility notes.
- Fixture collection uses the yt-dlp executable, not python3 -m yt_dlp.
- No cookies, browser profile data, credentials, private account data, local usernames, large media, or unsanitized dumps are committed.
- Offline validation loads each fixture and asserts enough raw fields exist to inform future VideoResult, Comment, and TimedTextSegment normalization.
- Tests run without network once fixtures are present.
- If BiliBili comments, danmaku, or subtitles are unavailable without auth or unsupported by yt-dlp, that limitation is recorded clearly and the fixture test reflects the documented limitation.

Testing:
- Add an offline fixture validation command or test file under skills/video-research/tests/ or the closest scaffold test location.
- Run the focused fixture validation test.
- Run scripts/validate-skill.sh skills/video-research/ only if this ticket creates or changes the skill directory.

Out of scope:
- Implementing sm-video normalization.
- Adding live network tests to CI.
- Adding cookies or authenticated provider support.
- Building comments/search/probe commands.

title: Document video-research skill migration from direct yt-dlp
description:
Create documentation for the new video-research skill contract and migration path while preserving existing YouTube workflows. This is a docs/skill-contract ticket, not a sm-video implementation ticket.

Expected edits:
- Create skills/video-research/SKILL.md if it does not already exist.
- Add CONFIG.yaml and MEMO.md only if the skill scaffold from skillmonger-k74.2 has not already created them.
- Optionally update skills/youtube/SKILL.md or references only to point to video-research as future cross-platform contract.

Start here:
- docs/video-research-direction.md sections Naming, Sensors And Actuators, First Feature, Migration, and Guardrails.
- skills/youtube/SKILL.md for existing umbrella workflow.
- skills/youtube/references/sensor-actuator-map.md and skills/youtube/references/workflows.md for existing YouTube behavior.
- AGENTS.md validation constraints.

Discovery:
- rg -n "youtube|youtube-search|youtube-clip|yt-dlp|comments|download|clip|dry-run|ffmpeg" skills/youtube skills/youtube-search skills/youtube-clip skills/yt-dlp docs -g "!vendor/**"
- rg --files skills | rg "video-research|youtube/.+md|yt-dlp/.+md"

Scratchpad:
Use docs/video-research-scratchpad.md for migration questions. Only durable, reviewed guidance belongs in SKILL.md files.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance:
- skills/video-research/SKILL.md has valid YAML frontmatter with name: video-research and a clear one-line description.
- The video-research skill lists sensors and actuators from docs/video-research-direction.md and marks actuators as requiring explicit user approval and --dry-run before non-dry-run execution.
- The docs state that new cross-platform video workflows should prefer sm-video once available.
- The docs state that existing youtube, youtube-search, youtube-clip, and yt-dlp skills remain compatibility workflows until migration tickets explicitly change them.
- The docs say new implementation should call yt-dlp executable, not python3 -m yt_dlp.
- ffmpeg is documented as optional for read-only workflows and required for clip/audio/merge/remux/recoding/subtitle embedding/chapter operations.
- No deployed copies under .claude/skills or yolobox directories are edited directly.
- Run scripts/validate-skill.sh skills/video-research/ and scripts/validate-skill.sh skills/youtube/ if youtube docs changed.

Out of scope:
- Implementing sm-video commands.
- Refactoring existing scripts to call sm-video.
- Changing deployed skill copies directly.
- Adding BiliBili implementation.

title: Add fixture-backed probe sensor for YouTube and BiliBili
description:
Add the provider-neutral probe sensor to the existing sm-video scaffold. Scope this ticket to individual video URL metadata for fixture-backed YouTube and BiliBili examples. Do not attempt arbitrary yt-dlp URL support, search, comments, transcripts, danmaku, downloads, clips, or migration docs.

Command shape:
sm-video probe --url URL [--fields id,title,uploader,uploader_id,duration,view_count,like_count,comment_count,upload_date,thumbnail,has_subtitles]

Start here:
- docs/video-research-direction.md sections Sensors And Actuators, Envelope, VideoResult schema, Prerequisites, and Guardrails.
- bd show skillmonger-k74.2 --json for the CLI scaffold.
- bd show skillmonger-k74.7 --json for raw fixture characterization.

Discovery:
- rg -n "probe|VideoResult|yt-dlp|dump-json|fields|provider" docs skills scripts -g "!vendor/**"
- yt-dlp --help | rg -n "dump-json|dump-single-json|skip-download|flat-playlist"

Scratchpad:
Use /private/tmp/sm-video-probe/ for raw experiments. Commit only sanitized fixtures. Record provider-specific limitations in the fixture manifest or docs/video-research-direction.md.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance:
- Depends on skillmonger-k74.2. Prefer using fixtures from skillmonger-k74.7.
- sm-video probe --describe returns structured command metadata and does not need network.
- sm-video probe --url YOUTUBE_URL returns one VideoResult object in data with meta.provider youtube and meta.source yt-dlp.
- sm-video probe --url BILIBILI_URL returns one VideoResult object in data with meta.provider bilibili and meta.source yt-dlp, using fixture-backed tests.
- --fields filters only the VideoResult data object.
- Unsupported URL families and malformed URLs return failure envelopes.
- Provider detection is limited to explicit supported URL families needed by this ticket.
- The implementation invokes yt-dlp executable paths only, with no python3 -m yt_dlp.
- Tests run offline using sanitized fixtures.

Testing:
- Add offline tests for YouTube and BiliBili metadata fixture normalization.
- Test missing optional fields are handled consistently.
- Test field masks, malformed URL handling, unsupported provider handling, and simulated yt-dlp failure.
- Run the focused video-research test command and scripts/validate-skill.sh skills/video-research/.

Out of scope:
- Search providers.
- Comments, transcripts, subtitles, or danmaku.
- Arbitrary extractor support beyond the supported URL families.
- Live network tests.
- Download or clip behavior.

title: Build sm-video agent-facing video research facade
description:
Create a provider-neutral CLI facade over yt-dlp for agent video research workflows. Canonical design: docs/video-research-direction.md.

Implementation target:
- Create skills/video-research/.
- Add executable skills/video-research/scripts/sm-video.
- Add skills/video-research/scripts/check-prereqs.sh.
- Keep fixtures/tests under skills/video-research/tests/ or skills/video-research/fixtures/.
- Do not commit cookies, browser profiles, credentials, private URLs, large media, or unsanitized dumps.
- Existing youtube, youtube-search, youtube-clip, and yt-dlp skills must keep working until a later migration explicitly changes them.

Execution order:
1. Scaffold CLI, envelope, --describe, prereqs, field masks, validation, and path hardening.
2. Characterize yt-dlp raw outputs with sanitized offline fixtures.
3. Implement YouTube comments as the first real sensor.
4. Add probe/search/transcript sensors after the envelope and fixtures are stable.
5. Document video-research skill migration.
6. Add BiliBili in narrow slices after YouTube is stable.
7. Add actuators last, with dry-run and approval rules first.

Discovery:
- rg -n "sm-video|video-research|VideoResult|Comment|TimedTextSegment|yt-dlp|ffmpeg" docs skills scripts -g "!vendor/**"
- rg --files | rg "video-research|youtube|yt-dlp|tests|fixtures"

Scratchpad:
Use docs/video-research-direction.md as the durable design reference. Put temporary upstream notes or unresolved findings in docs/video-research-scratchpad.md and only promote durable decisions back into the design doc.
acceptance_criteria:
Epic is complete when a zero-context agent can read the child tickets and build the first shippable video-research skill without guessing file locations, command contracts, dependencies, or safety expectations.

Required outcome:
- skills/video-research/SKILL.md exists with valid frontmatter and sensor/actuator guidance.
- skills/video-research/scripts/sm-video exposes --describe and returns one JSON envelope shape for success and failure.
- check-prereqs reports yt-dlp as required and ffmpeg as optional with required_for capabilities.
- Read-only sensors land before actuators.
- The first real sensor is YouTube comments search.
- Tests or fixtures run without network for normalization paths.
- New implementation calls the yt-dlp executable, not python3 -m yt_dlp.
- Actuators require --dry-run planning and explicit user approval before non-dry-run execution.
- Existing YouTube skills validate and remain compatible during migration.
- Ticket text for remaining work includes onboarding, scratchpad, testing, acceptance, dependencies, and out-of-scope boundaries.

title: Import new-project-triage skill into skillmonger
description: Bring ~/.claude/skills/new-project-triage under skillmonger/skills source control, validate it, deploy it through skillmonger, and sync/commit the result.
close_reason: Imported new-project-triage into skills/, fixed deploy replacement for existing global directories, validated, and deployed globally.

title: Add --describe flag to youtube-search scripts
description:
Agents cannot discover the script interface at runtime. Add --describe flag that emits machine-readable JSON schema and exits immediately.

## Files to modify

1. skills/youtube-search/scripts/search
   - Add --describe flag to argparse (near line 106, with other flags)
   - After args = parser.parse_args() (line 109), add early exit:
     if args.describe: <emit schema and sys.exit(0)>
   - output_fields: must match normalize_entry() return dict keys exactly (lines 82-93):
     id, title, channel, channel_id, duration, view_count, upload_date, url, thumbnail, description_snippet (10 fields)
   - flags: enumerate all argparse arguments (query, limit, offset, filter, date, type, subtitles_only, channel_url, pretty, help_examples)

2. skills/youtube-search/scripts/deep-dive
   - Add --describe flag to argparse (near line 114)
   - After args = parser.parse_args() (line 116), add early exit
   - output_fields: must match normalize_video() return dict keys exactly (lines 86-108):
     id, title, channel, channel_id, channel_followers, duration, view_count, like_count, comment_count, upload_date, categories, tags, description, url, thumbnail, chapters, heatmap, captions (18 fields)
   - flags: enumerate argparse arguments (video, pretty)

3. skills/youtube-search/scripts/evaluate
   - evaluate uses manual arg parsing (lines 111-114), NOT argparse
   - Add check before existing --help handling at line 112:
     if len(sys.argv) >= 2 and sys.argv[1] == "--describe": <emit schema and sys.exit(0)>
   - output_fields: outcome, note, checks (from lines 234-245)
   - flags: file (positional/optional), --help

## Schema shape

{
  "name": "<script-name>",
  "version": "<from CONFIG.yaml>",
  "flags": [
    {"name": "<arg-name>", "type": "<string|int|bool|choice>", "required": <bool>, "default": <value|null>, "description": "<one-line>"}
  ],
  "output_fields": [
    {"name": "<field-name>", "type": "<string|int|list|dict|null>", "description": "<one-line>"}
  ]
}

For version: hardcode to match CONFIG.yaml version.

## API boundary

Adding --describe as a new public flag. No existing behavior changed.

## Acceptance criteria (run from skills/youtube-search/)

1. ./scripts/search --describe | jq '.name' returns "search"
2. ./scripts/search --describe | jq '.flags | length' returns positive integer
3. ./scripts/search --describe | jq '.output_fields | map(.name)' returns ["id","title","channel","channel_id","duration","view_count","upload_date","url","thumbnail","description_snippet"]
4. ./scripts/deep-dive --describe | jq '.output_fields | length' returns 18
5. ./scripts/evaluate --describe | jq '.name' returns "evaluate"
6. --describe exits 0 in <1 second with no network call
close_reason: Added --describe schemas to youtube-search search, deep-dive, and evaluate; QA passed schema acceptance checks, py_compile, validate-skill, and diff check.

title: Add --describe flag to scientific-paper-searcher scripts
description:
Agents cannot discover the script interface at runtime. Add --describe flag that emits machine-readable JSON schema and exits immediately (no API calls).

## Files to modify

1. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-s2
   Add --describe handling in the arg parser (around line 21, as the FIRST case in the while loop, before the --*) catch-all). When --describe is seen, emit JSON and exit 0 immediately, before the missing-query check on line 33.

2. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-pubmed
   Same approach. Add before the catch-all on line 22, before missing-query check on line 27.

3. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-arxiv
   Same approach. Add before the catch-all on line 18, before missing-query check on line 23.

4. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-crossref
   Same approach. Add before the catch-all on line 20, before missing-query check on line 25.

5. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/lookup-s2
   Same approach. Add before the catch-all on line 36, before missing-id check on line 41.

6. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search (orchestrator)
   Add before the catch-all on line 29, before missing-query check on line 34.

## Schema shape

{
  "name": "<script-name>",
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "flags": [
    {"name": "query", "type": "string", "required": true, "description": "Search query text"},
    {"name": "--limit", "type": "int", "required": false, "default": 20, "description": "Max results to return"}
  ],
  "output_fields": [
    {"name": "title", "type": "string", "description": "Paper title"}
  ]
}

For version: hardcode "2.0.0" to match CONFIG.yaml line 5. Do not read CONFIG.yaml at runtime.
For output_fields: list the NORMALIZED output keys from each script's jq template. For search-s2 these are: title, authors, year, citations, doi, url, open_access_url, abstract, source_id.

CRITICAL: --describe must be handled BEFORE the if [[ -z "$QUERY" ]] / if [[ -z "$PAPER_ID" ]] guard, since --describe runs without a query. Put the case branch first and exit immediately.

## API boundary

Adding --describe as a new public flag to each script. No existing behavior changed.

## Acceptance criteria

1. cd skills/scientific-paper-searcher && ./scripts/search --describe | jq '.flags | length' returns a positive integer
2. cd skills/scientific-paper-searcher && ./scripts/search-s2 --describe | jq '.output_fields | map(.name)' returns list containing "title" and "abstract" and "source_id"
3. cd skills/scientific-paper-searcher && ./scripts/search-pubmed --describe | jq '.name' returns "search-pubmed"
4. cd skills/scientific-paper-searcher && ./scripts/lookup-s2 --describe | jq '.name' returns "lookup-s2"
5. All six scripts: ./scripts/<name> --describe exits 0 and emits valid JSON (test with jq .)
6. ./scripts/search-s2 --describe | jq '.version' returns "2.0.0"
close_reason: Added early --describe JSON schemas to all six scientific-paper-searcher public scripts; QA passed bash syntax, all jq schema checks, validate-skill, and diff check.

title: Add --fields flag to youtube-search scripts
description:
Agents always receive all fields (10 for search, 18 for deep-dive). Add --fields to reduce context window waste.

## Files to modify

1. skills/youtube-search/scripts/search
   - Add --fields argument near line 106 (after --channel-url)
   - Filter normalize_entry() output in main() after line 213
   - Actual search fields (from normalize_entry return dict, lines 82-93):
     id, title, channel, channel_id, duration, view_count, upload_date, url, thumbnail, description_snippet (10 fields)

2. skills/youtube-search/scripts/deep-dive
   - Add --fields argument near line 114 (after --pretty)
   - Filter normalize_video() output in main() after line 176
   - Actual deep-dive fields (from normalize_video return dict, lines 86-108):
     id, title, channel, channel_id, channel_followers, duration, view_count, like_count, comment_count, upload_date, categories, tags, description, url, thumbnail, chapters, heatmap, captions (18 fields)

3. skills/youtube-search/SKILL.md
   - Add --fields to the flag table (lines 36-42)

## Implementation approach

Filter AFTER normalize_*, not inside it. Keep normalize functions producing full output, then strip keys not in --fields. This preserves the normalize functions as the schema contract.
If --fields is not provided, return all fields (backward compatible).
If a field name is not recognized, silently ignore it (no error).

## API boundary

Adding --fields to the CLI interface of search and deep-dive. Optional, backward-compatible.

## Acceptance criteria (run from skills/youtube-search/)

1. Search with --fields returns only specified fields:
   ./scripts/search "photosynthesis" --limit 3 --fields id,title | jq '.data[0] | keys | sort'
   -> ["id", "title"]
2. Search without --fields returns all 10 fields:
   ./scripts/search "photosynthesis" --limit 1 | jq '.data[0] | keys | length'
   -> 10
3. Deep-dive with --fields works:
   ./scripts/deep-dive "rQ_J9WH6CGk" --fields id,title,like_count | jq '.data | keys | sort'
   -> ["id", "like_count", "title"]
4. Deep-dive without --fields returns all 18 fields:
   ./scripts/deep-dive "rQ_J9WH6CGk" | jq '.data | keys | length'
   -> 18
5. From project root: scripts/validate-skill.sh skills/youtube-search/ passes
close_reason: Added --fields to youtube-search search and deep-dive with schema/docs updates; QA passed live field-filter checks, validate-skill, py_compile, and diff check.

title: Validate --channel-url input in youtube-search
description:
The search script passes --channel-url directly to yt-dlp with no validation (lines 130-132). Add URL validation.

## File to modify

skills/youtube-search/scripts/search
- Line 105: --channel-url arg definition
- Lines 130-132: channel_url is used raw in string interpolation
- Add validation after args = parser.parse_args() (line 109), before start = time.time() (line 115)
- Valid: must start with https://www.youtube.com/ or https://youtube.com/ or https://m.youtube.com/
- On invalid: print JSON error envelope and sys.exit(1), matching existing error pattern at lines 167-175

## Acceptance criteria (run from skills/youtube-search/)

1. Valid channel URL works:
   ./scripts/search "test" --channel-url "https://www.youtube.com/@GoogleDevelopers" --limit 1
   -> success:true in JSON output
2. Invalid URL produces JSON error:
   ./scripts/search "test" --channel-url "https://evil.com/channel" --limit 1
   -> success:false with error mentioning "channel-url", exit code 1
3. No --channel-url unchanged:
   ./scripts/search "test" --limit 1
   -> success:true, no channel validation attempted
4. Edge: bare youtube.com path works:
   ./scripts/search "test" --channel-url "https://youtube.com/@GoogleDevelopers" --limit 1
   -> success:true
close_reason: Added --channel-url prefix validation to youtube-search search; QA passed invalid/valid URL behavior, validate-skill, py_compile, and diff check.

title: Add --fields flag to scientific-paper-searcher scripts
description:
Agents cannot suppress large fields (especially abstract) when they only need metadata for dedup or ranking. Add --fields to the search scripts to reduce context window consumption.

## Files to modify

1. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-s2
   - Line 17: API_FIELDS="title,authors,year,citationCount,abstract,url,externalIds,openAccessPdf" is hardcoded
   - Add --fields to arg parser (around line 25). When provided, use as the S2 API fields query param. ALSO filter jq output (lines 92-108) to only include requested keys, since S2 API field names (e.g. citationCount) differ from normalized output keys (e.g. citations).

2. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-pubmed
   - Lines 67-85: jq normalization hardcodes output shape
   - PubMed API has no field-selection parameter, so filtering is purely post-processing via jq

3. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-arxiv
   - Lines 85-104: jq normalization hardcodes output shape (same post-processing approach as pubmed)

4. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-crossref
   - Lines 61-77: jq normalization hardcodes output shape (same post-processing approach as pubmed)

5. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search (orchestrator)
   - Lines 14-31: arg parser. Add --fields) PASSTHROUGH_ARGS+=("$1" "$2"); shift 2 ;; to the case statement (around line 25).

6. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/lookup-s2
   - ALREADY HAS --fields (line 34). No change needed. Not in scope.

## Implementation approach

For all scripts, add a post-processing filter function:
  filter_fields() {
    local fields="$1"
    if [[ -z "$fields" ]]; then
      cat  # pass through unchanged
    else
      jq --arg fields "$fields" 'if type == "object" and .results then .results |= [.[] | with_entries(select(.key as $k | ($fields | split(",") | index($k))))] else with_entries(select(.key as $k | ($fields | split(",") | index($k)))) end'
    fi
  }

Pipe final output through filter_fields "$FIELDS".

The --fields values must match NORMALIZED output key names (title, authors, year, citations, doi, url, open_access_url, abstract, source_id), not upstream API field names.

## API boundary

Adding --fields to the CLI interface of each search script. The flag is optional and backward-compatible (no --fields = all fields).

## Acceptance criteria

1. cd skills/scientific-paper-searcher && ./scripts/search-s2 "transformer" --limit 3 --fields title,year,citations | jq '.results[0] | keys | sort' returns ["citations","title","year"]
2. cd skills/scientific-paper-searcher && ./scripts/search-s2 "transformer" --limit 3 (no --fields) | jq '.results[0] | has("abstract")' returns true
3. cd skills/scientific-paper-searcher && ./scripts/search "attention" --databases s2 --limit 3 --fields title,doi | jq '.results[0] | keys | sort' returns ["doi","title"]
4. From project root: scripts/validate-skill.sh skills/scientific-paper-searcher/ passes (exit 0)
close_reason: Added --fields to scientific-paper-searcher search scripts and orchestrator with describe updates; QA passed S2/orchestrator field projection checks, bash syntax, validate-skill, and diff check.

title: Add --offset pagination to scientific-paper-searcher scripts
description:
Agents cannot page beyond the first result set. Add --offset to all search scripts for pagination.

## Files to modify

1. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-pubmed
   - Add OFFSET=0 default (after line 10, near other defaults)
   - Add --offset) OFFSET="$2"; shift 2 ;; to arg parser (around line 19)
   - Add &retstart=${OFFSET} to ESEARCH_URL (line 39)

2. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-arxiv
   - Add OFFSET=0 default (after line 9)
   - Add --offset) OFFSET="$2"; shift 2 ;; to arg parser (around line 17)
   - Add &start=${OFFSET} to URL (line 36)

3. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-crossref
   - Add OFFSET=0 default (after line 10)
   - Add --offset) OFFSET="$2"; shift 2 ;; to arg parser (around line 19)
   - Add &offset=${OFFSET} to URL (line 41)

4. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search-s2
   - Add OFFSET=0 default (after line 14)
   - Add --offset) OFFSET="$2"; shift 2 ;; to arg parser (around line 27)
   - IMPORTANT: S2 has TWO pagination models:
     a) Relevance endpoint (line 47): supports offset param directly. Add &offset=${OFFSET} when OFFSET>0.
     b) Bulk endpoint (lines 50, 53): does NOT support offset. Uses cursor token pagination. For bulk mode, ignore --offset and emit a note in output. A future ticket can add cursor-based pagination.
   - Do NOT attempt to add offset to bulk endpoint URLs.

5. skills/scientific-paper-searcher/scripts/search (orchestrator)
   - Add --offset) PASSTHROUGH_ARGS+=("$1" "$2"); shift 2 ;; to arg parser (around line 25)

## Implementation notes

- All scripts should include offset in their JSON output. Add an "offset" field at the top level (alongside database, query, total, etc.).
- Default offset is 0 (backward compatible).

## API boundary

Adding --offset to the CLI interface of each search script. Optional, backward-compatible.

## Acceptance criteria

1. cd skills/scientific-paper-searcher && ./scripts/search-pubmed "cancer" --limit 5 --offset 5 | jq '.offset' returns 5
2. cd skills/scientific-paper-searcher && ./scripts/search-arxiv "quantum" --limit 3 --offset 3 | jq '.offset' returns 3
3. cd skills/scientific-paper-searcher && ./scripts/search-crossref "climate" --limit 5 --offset 10 | jq '.offset' returns 10
4. cd skills/scientific-paper-searcher && ./scripts/search-s2 "attention" --limit 3 --sort relevance --offset 3 | jq '.offset' returns 3
5. cd skills/scientific-paper-searcher && ./scripts/search-s2 "attention" --limit 3 (no offset, default sort=citations/bulk) returns first page as before
6. cd skills/scientific-paper-searcher && ./scripts/search "transformer" --databases pubmed --limit 3 --offset 3 | jq '.sources[0].offset' returns 3
7. Default offset is 0: each script without --offset includes "offset":0 in output
close_reason: Added --offset to scientific-paper-searcher search scripts and orchestrator with source offsets and describe updates; QA passed all offset acceptance checks, default offset checks, validate-skill, bash syntax, and diff check.

title: Add sm-video download and clip actuators with dry-run planning
description:
Implement only the sm-video download and sm-video clip actuator commands on top of the existing sm-video CLI scaffold. This ticket adds actuator command parsing, dry-run planning, CWD-sandboxed output path validation, ffmpeg-aware prerequisite reporting, and focused tests.

Do not implement the full explore actuator in this ticket. sm-video explore needs a separate ticket because it involves HTML generation plus transcript and probe integration.

Start here:
- docs/video-research-direction.md sections Naming, Sensors And Actuators, Envelope, Prerequisites, and Guardrails.
- skills/youtube/SKILL.md for current actuator approval expectations.
- skills/youtube/references/workflows.md for current download and clip workflow behavior.
- skills/youtube-clip/scripts/explore only as prior art for a future explore actuator. Do not port it here.

Discovery:
- rg -n "sm-video|video-research|download|clip|dry-run|ffmpeg|yt-dlp" . -g "!vendor/**"
- rg --files | rg "sm-video|video-research|youtube|yt-dlp|tests|fixtures"

Scratchpad:
Use docs/video-research-scratchpad.md for unresolved actuator planning notes. Promote only durable safety rules back to docs/video-research-direction.md or skill docs.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance:
- Requires skillmonger-k74.2. Requires skillmonger-k74.4 if skill docs need actuator approval updates.
- sm-video download --url URL --mode video|audio|metadata|subtitles --output-dir DIR --dry-run returns the standard JSON envelope and does not write files.
- sm-video clip --url URL --start START --end END --output-dir DIR --dry-run returns the standard JSON envelope and does not write files.
- Dry-run output includes the resolved yt-dlp executable command, any required ffmpeg capability, resolved output directory, and prerequisite status.
- Non-dry-run download and clip are implemented only if the CLI architecture already supports safe execution. Otherwise they fail with a clear execution_not_enabled envelope while dry-run remains complete.
- clip reports success:false with a clear envelope when ffmpeg is unavailable.
- Output paths are canonicalized and rejected if absolute, contain traversal, contain unsafe percent-encoding, or resolve outside the current working directory.
- The implementation calls yt-dlp executable paths only, with no python3 -m yt_dlp.
- Skill docs or command descriptions mark download and clip as actuators requiring explicit user approval before non-dry-run execution.
- sm-video --describe includes both actuator commands and identifies them as file-writing or heavy operations requiring --dry-run.

Testing:
- Add unit tests for dry-run JSON envelopes for download and clip.
- Test path sandboxing: safe relative path accepted, absolute path rejected, ../outside rejected, and encoded traversal rejected.
- Test ffmpeg-missing behavior by mocking prereq/path lookup.
- Test dry-run does not create files.
- Test generated planner commands call yt-dlp, not python3 -m yt_dlp.
- Run focused sm-video tests and scripts/validate-skill.sh skills/video-research/ if skill docs changed.

Out of scope:
- sm-video explore.
- BiliBili actuator behavior.
- Refactoring existing youtube, youtube-clip, or yt-dlp skills except for minimal documentation pointers required here.
- Network-dependent integration tests against live video providers.

title: Add BiliBili search sensor to sm-video
description:
Implement only the BiliBili branch of the read-only sm-video search sensor. The command accepts --provider bilibili --query TEXT --limit N --offset N --fields ..., calls the yt-dlp executable, and normalizes returned entries into the shared JSON envelope with VideoResult items.

This ticket does not implement BiliBili probe, comments, danmaku, subtitle download/parsing, media downloads, clips, explore pages, or skill migration docs.

Start here:
- docs/video-research-direction.md for the sm-video command contract, envelope, schemas, prerequisites, and guardrails.
- bd show skillmonger-k74.2 --json for the required CLI scaffold contract.
- bd show skillmonger-k74.7 --json for fixture and characterization expectations.

Discovery:
- rg -n "sm-video|VideoResult|TimedTextSegment|Comment|--describe|yt-dlp" . -g "!vendor/**"
- rg -n "youtube-search|yt-dlp|--default-search|search" skills scripts docs -g "!vendor/**"
- yt-dlp --list-extractors | rg -i "bili|bilibili"

Scratchpad:
Record BiliBili-specific upstream limitations or fixture notes in docs/video-research-scratchpad.md while working. Promote only durable limitations to docs/video-research-direction.md under a short BiliBili notes subsection.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance:
- Requires skillmonger-k74.2. Prefer completing skillmonger-k74.7 first if fixture shape is still unknown.
- sm-video search --provider bilibili --query "test" --limit 3 --offset 0 returns the standard envelope with success, data, and meta.
- Each data item conforms to the VideoResult schema fields available from yt-dlp. Unavailable optional fields are null or omitted consistently with the existing facade convention.
- meta.provider is bilibili, meta.source is yt-dlp, and meta.schema_version is present.
- --limit, --offset, --fields, and --describe work for the BiliBili search path.
- Invalid provider values are rejected at the CLI boundary with a failure envelope.
- The implementation calls yt-dlp executable paths only, with no python3 -m yt_dlp.
- No command in this ticket writes media, subtitle, comment, credential, or browser-profile files.
- Known BiliBili search limitations are documented briefly in docs/video-research-direction.md.

Testing:
- Add fixture-based tests for BiliBili search normalization so tests run without network.
- Include at least one fixture containing missing optional fields.
- Test --fields filters normalized VideoResult output.
- Test invalid provider handling.
- Run the focused sm-video tests and scripts/validate-skill.sh skills/video-research/ if skill docs changed.

Out of scope:
- BiliBili probe normalization.
- BiliBili comments normalization.
- Danmaku parsing or conversion to TimedTextSegment.
- Subtitle listing/download/parsing.
- Login or cookie handling.
- Download, clip, or explore actuators.
- Refactoring existing YouTube skills to use sm-video.

Suggested follow-ups:
- Add BiliBili comments sensor normalization.
- Add BiliBili danmaku/subtitle timed-text normalization.
- Document BiliBili login/cookie limitations after characterization.
